RBI/2023-24/99 DCM (CC) No.G-5/03.41.01/2023-24 April 03, 2023
The Chairman/Managing Director/Chief Executive Officers, All banks
Madam / Dear Sir,
Master Direction on Framework of Incentives for Currency Distribution & Exchange Scheme for bank branches including currency chests based on performance in rendering customer service to the members of public
In terms of the Preamble to and Section 45 of the RBI Act, 1934 and Section 35 A of

hierarchy.title: Withdrawn
hierarchy.text:
Withdrawn
the Banking Regulation Act, 1949; Reserve Bank of India issues guidelines /
instructions for realising the objectives of Clean Note Policy as part of currency
management. With a view to furthering these objectives, the Bank has formulated a
framework of incentives titled Currency Distribution and Exchange Scheme (CDES) to
encourage all the bank branches to provide better customer services to the members
of public.

hierarchy.title: 2. The enclosed Master Direction incorporates updated guidelines / circulars on the
hierarchy.text:
2. The enclosed Master Direction incorporates updated guidelines / circulars on the
subject.
Yours faithfully,
(Sanjeev Prakash) Chief General Manager
Encl: As above

hierarchy.title: Annex
hierarchy.text:
Annex
Master Direction on Framework of Incentives for "Currency Distribution & Exchange Scheme (CDES)" for bank branches including currency chests based on performance in rendering customer service to the members of public

hierarchy.title: 1. The framework of incentives titled Currency Distribution & Exchange Scheme
hierarchy.text:
1. The framework of incentives titled Currency Distribution & Exchange Scheme
(CDES) for bank branches including currency chests has been formulated to
encourage all the bank branches to provide better customer service to the members
of public keeping in view the objectives of Clean Note Policy.

hierarchy.title: 2. Incentives
hierarchy.text:
2. Incentives
As per the scheme, banks are eligible for the following financial incentives/service
charges for setting up requisite infrastructure and facilitating exchange/distribution of

children.title: notes and coins:

notes and coins:

revenue expenditure for the first 3 years (inclusive
of all taxes). In the North Eastern region, 50% of
revenue expenditure is eligible for reimbursement
for the first 5 years (inclusive of all taxes).

hierarchy.title: 3. Other operational instructions on payment of performance-based incentives
hierarchy.text:
3. Other operational instructions on payment of performance-based incentives
i.
The incentives shall be paid on the soiled notes actually received in the Issue

hierarchy.title: Withdrawn
hierarchy.text:
Withdrawn
Office of the RBI.
ii.
Incentive shall be paid in respect of the adjudicated notes received along with
the soiled note remittances or sent separately by registered / insured post in a
sealed cover to the RBI.
iii.
The incentive for distribution of coins shall be paid on the basis of net withdrawal
from the currency chest.
iv.
Banks do not need to submit a separate claim for the incentives. Currency chest
branch shall pass on the incentive to the linked branches/chests for the soiled

children.title: notes tendered / coins distributed/ mutilated notes adjudicated by them on pro-
children.text:
notes tendered / coins distributed/ mutilated notes adjudicated by them on pro-
rata basis.
v.
The distribution of coins shall be verified by RBI Regional Offices through
inspection of currency chest / incognito visits to branches, etc.
vi.
Instructions issued vide circular DCM(CC) No.97527/03.41.01/2021-22 dated
August 27, 2021 remains unchanged.
1 Large modern CCs are those CCs which fulfil the Minimum Standards for a CC as detailed
in the circular RBI/2018-19/166 DCM(CC) No. 2842/03.39.01/2018-19 dated April 08, 2019.
